Enter advanced settings. Some settings may be required by your email provider and others can be used to adapt email to your needs.
Select to automatically receive new messages (when enabled in Check interval or Push email) only when in your home network. Contact your network operator for cost information regarding networks.

Select for an online connection and receive new messages as they arrive on the mail server. Check interval setting should be Off.

Push email gives instant email download, but can be expensive.
Select how much of a message is downloaded, e.g. subject only or up to a size limit. Note: Downloading large messages may be expensive. Contact your network operator for more information.
Select how often to automatically check for new messages.

Select a specific data account from your network operator to connect using 3G, GPRS or GSM data. If no account exists, contact your network operator to receive it automatically or for more information. You should not have to create it manually.
Copy outgoing messages automatically to a specified email address.
You must select a data account.
Enter your address for email, for example john@example.com. This information is available from your email provider.
Change the alert sound for new messages. The same alert is used for all email accounts.
Use if your incoming or outgoing server requires a secure connection, (SSL or TLS). This information is available from your email provider.

You must add your email address in Settings.
Set for recipients to see your name together with your email address, for example John Smith [john@example.com].
Download only the subjects of new messages. You can then read the subject before downloading the complete message. Useful if you do not want to read all new messages.
Incoming server port number. Use the default unless otherwise instructed by your email provider.
Enter your incoming (POP3 or IMAP4) mail server name, for example mail.example.com. This information is available from your email provider.
Note: Using short check intervals can be expensive. Please contact your service provider for cost information.
Enter your account username (sometimes referred to as mailbox or incoming username). This information is available from your email provider.
Enter your account password (sometimes referred to as mailbox or incoming password). This information is available from your email provider.

Open page to configure email settings for your different accounts.

Outgoing server port number. Use the default unless otherwise instructed by your email provider.

Enter your outgoing (SMTP) mail server name, for example smtp.example.com. This may be the same server as for incoming mail. If separate username and password are required, see Advanced settings. This information is available from your email provider.
Enter the connection type of your incoming server, POP3 or IMAP4. This information is available from your email provider.

For multitap text input, press a key as many times as needed to show the character you want to enter. For example, press 7 four times to enter an 's'. Switch between capital and lower-case letters with the * key.
To change writing method, press and hold the * key. To change writing languages, press and hold the # key.

For T9 text input, press each key once only for each letter of the word you want to write. Write the whole word before looking at the suggestions displayed on the screen. Select the desired word. Press More and use multitap text input to write a word not

You can add the currently playing track to your wish list. The track can then be purchased and downloaded to your phone via the Vodafone Music Store.
You can buy and download tracks to your phone via the Vodafone Music Store.
These are predefined music channels. To rate tracks in the Radio DJ channels, you will be prompted to create your own copy of the channel. This will be placed in My channels.
Radio DJ channels are predefined music channels. Radio DJ channels have an unlimited number of tracks and you can skip to the next or previous track.
A collection is a predefined limited number of tracks, for example, an album. You cannot rate the tracks in collections.
Collections have a predefined limited number of tracks.
You can delete a channel in My channels by either choosing this option or using the C key as a shortcut.

Radio DJ is an interactive, personalized music service available on your phone and PC that gives you instant access to the music you want now! Listen to your favourite tracks, discover new music and create personal channels with an easy-to-use, intuitive
You can enter here to purchase listening time for your account. You need valid listening time to listen to channels and collections.

You can define, create and listen to your own music channels. In My channels, you can rate tracks as like or dislike. When you rate a track, the system will learn your music taste over time and will personalize your channels. If you dislike a track, the s
My channels are your personally defined and created music channels. My channels have an unlimited number of tracks and you can skip to the next or previous track.
Choose this option to add a new personal channel.

When playing a channel or collection, you can skip to the next or previous track by pressing the navigation key left or right. Press the navigation key to switch between play and pause modes. Move the navigation key up as a shortcut to rate the currently

When you rate a track, the system will learn your music taste over time and will personalize your channels. If you dislike a track, the system stops playing that particular track and starts the next track in the list.
Use this to rename a channel in My channels.
A collection is a predefined limited number of tracks, for example, an album. You can start playing any of the tracks in a collection.
You can view and purchase tracks from your wish list at the Vodafone Music Store. When listening to a channel or a collection, you can add the currently playing track to your wish list.
